title: apium graveolens subsp. butronense
intro: Apium graveolens subsp. butronense, also known as the Galician celery, is a subspecies of the wild celery plant, characterized by its distinctive flavor and aroma. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can be attributed to the presence of various compounds such as limonene and beta-pinene. The health benefits of Apium graveolens subsp. butronense include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and essential oils, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, from digestive problems to skin conditions.
